title: When Recalling Events: - Use Evidence: Look at Photos, Reports, or Other Objective Records
subtitle: Reduce Egocentric Bias
description:
When recalling events:
- Use evidence: Look at photos, reports, or other objective records of what happened.
- Ask others: Gather perspectives from those who were there.
- Reflect honestly: Recognize moments where your memory might exaggerate your role or success.

Example: If you remember organizing a successful event, check with others to confirm your recollection.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Record how often you make claims based on memory without checking. Estimate the time spent correcting or clarifying later to establish a baseline for improvement.

howTo.title: Perform the recall check
howTo.summary: When recalling an event, spend up to 10 minutes searching for one objective cue, such as a recording, photo, or message, to confirm your memory.

howTo.title: Log your findings
howTo.summary: After checking, log your findings in the Brali LifeOS app, noting the date, claim, evidence found, or if no evidence was available.

howTo.title: Review and adjust claims
howTo.summary: If evidence contradicts your memory, adjust your claims accordingly. Use precise language to maintain credibility and clarity in communication.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and refine your memory-checking habit. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/reduce-egocentric-bias-with-evidence/.
